remediation_update: exception_debt_001 through exception_debt_005 have been removed from route-specific code branches in the active exporter/audit path and converted to data-backed hints or generic checks.
purpose: Inventory code-level route exceptions that could hide reusable BTC heuristics.

findings.current_behavior: Adds Harrison-specific field notes for repeated #57 / #51 / #52 junctions and the 1B overlap.
findings.risk: The same confusing-junction pattern can happen anywhere a route revisits a signed junction cluster.
findings.durable_shape: Keep field-tested notes as data and generate caution prompts from repeated junctions, dense signpost clusters, and overlap/exit topology.
findings.remediation_status: field-tested prose moved to years/2026/inputs/personal/2026-field-route-hints.json; generic overlap warnings remain generated from geometry

findings.current_behavior: Hardcodes Harrison Hollow Trailhead to Who Now access via #57 Harrison Hollow (AWT).
findings.risk: Named non-credit access from car to first official segment is a general field-readiness requirement, not a Harrison-only fact.
findings.durable_shape: Infer named start/return access from the connector graph and signpost labels; store fallback route-access hints in data/config rather than code.
findings.remediation_status: route-specific code removed; fallback access hints now live in data/config and route-line matched names are still added generically

findings.current_behavior: Blocks one known collapsed Package 1 / block-hillside_harrison_frontside regression.
findings.risk: Accepted split and multi-start replacements can disappear anywhere after recalculation, not only Package 1.
findings.durable_shape: Certification should generically assert that accepted replacement packages/candidates from the current manifest remain present or are explicitly superseded.
findings.remediation_status: replaced with accepted replacement manifest preservation keyed by package plus block identity; long single-card outings are allowed unless they supersede an accepted replacement

findings.current_behavior: Adds a Harrison-specific double-back and exit warning for cue 7 to 8.
findings.risk: The generic overlap detector now catches repeated-line legs, but overlap exit guidance is still tied to one route label and cue number.
findings.durable_shape: Extend generic overlap analysis to emit exit-transition warnings when an overlap leg is followed by a distinct signed edge.
findings.remediation_status: replaced with apply_overlap_exit_wayfinding_cautions keyed off overlap_match direction and next cue

findings.current_behavior: Checks that 1B includes #57 Harrison Hollow (AWT) before Who Now and on return after Hippie Shake.
findings.risk: The audit proves one route but not the general rule that named non-credit access/return edges must appear in cues.
findings.durable_shape: Compare matched non-credit named edges against cue/step text for every route and fail missing names generically.
findings.remediation_status: replaced with generic named start-access and return-access cue checks

findings.current_behavior: Reports whether one West Climb candidate id is found after audit.
findings.risk: Candidate-specific summary metrics can hide whether all accepted replacement classes are preserved.
findings.durable_shape: Replace with generic accepted-candidate / promising-candidate preservation metrics keyed by audit-selected alternatives.

findings.current_behavior: Rewrites two private-anchor candidate ids to public-safe labels.
findings.risk: Public sanitization labels are data, not route logic; string matching candidate ids can silently miss new private anchors.
findings.durable_shape: Store public-safe labels in parking-review or replacement metadata and make sanitizer consume that metadata.

findings.current_behavior: Restricts Bogus-area components to known lodge/trailhead anchors.
findings.risk: This is a valid local-reality policy, but it currently lives as code constants rather than a sourced local-reality config.
findings.durable_shape: Move place-specific access policies into local-reality data/config with source notes, then have the audit consume that.

findings.current_behavior: Encodes Lower Hulls odd/even, Polecat/ATM/Bucktail signage checks, and low-foothills heat set in code.
findings.risk: These are real local rules, but they should be transparent data-backed local reality, not invisible code-only assumptions.
findings.durable_shape: Extract to a current local-reality rule file or official-condition rule table; keep code as evaluator only.

findings.current_behavior: Keeps a named Shingle exception scenario as a what-if diagnostic.
findings.risk: A named exception can leak into planning if not clearly isolated from active strict-profile routing.
findings.durable_shape: Keep as diagnostic-only unless the user explicitly changes the p90 policy; active planners should require a policy/config gate.

follow_up_backlog: Move public-safe private-anchor labels to replacement or parking-review metadata.
Move Bogus Basin and day-of named-trail policies into local-reality config consumed by preflight/audit scripts.
Replace West Climb-specific candidate summary metrics with generic accepted/promising-candidate preservation metrics.
Keep Shingle what-if scripts diagnostic-only unless a policy/config gate explicitly promotes them.
